message.content:
Perform a very thorough exploration of this MMS project structure. I need to understand:
1. The full directory tree and organization
2. Key entry points (package.json scripts, main files)
3. Configuration files (tsconfig, docker, etc.)
4. Dependencies and their versions
5. Build and deployment setup

Focus on giving me a complete picture of how this project is organized. Read package.json, tsconfig files, docker configs, and any infrastructure files.

content.text: I'll perform a thorough exploration of the MMS project structure. Let me start by mapping out the directory tree and then dive into key configuration files.

input.description: Find configuration files in the project

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["es5", "es6", "es7", "esnext", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "types": ["vite/client", "node", "bun"],
    "skipLibCheck": true,

    /* Bundler mode */
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "verbatimModuleSyntax": true,
    "moduleDetection": "force",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",

    /* Linting */
    "strict": true,
    "strictPropertyInitialization": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": false,
    "noUnusedParameters": false,
    "noImplicitAny": false,
    "noImplicitReturns": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "downlevelIteration": true,
    "isolatedModules": true
  },
  "include": [
    "src",
    "vite.config.ts",
    ".agentrc.ts"
  ]
}

    <App />
  </StrictMode>,
)

1вҶ’ 2вҶ’ 3вҶ’ 4вҶ’ 5вҶ’ 6вҶ’ 7вҶ’ MMS 8вҶ’ 9вҶ’ 10вҶ’
11вҶ’ 12вҶ’ 13вҶ’ 14вҶ’ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1вҶ’# MMS Demo Application 2вҶ’ 3вҶ’**Metro Fleet Mileage Management System** - Win-the-bid demonstration application. 4вҶ’ 5вҶ’## Overview 6вҶ’ 7вҶ’This is a demonstration application for Metro fleet preventive maintenance (PM) management. It showcases CSV-based PM projection over a 10-year horizon with an infinite canvas interface using semantic zoom levels (strategic вҶ’ tactical вҶ’ operational views). 8вҶ’ 9вҶ’**Key Features:** 10вҶ’- Drag-and-drop PM rescheduling with constraint validation 11вҶ’- "Bow-wave" risk visualization (fleet maintenance clustering) 12вҶ’- WebGL-powered infinite canvas rendering with deck.gl 13вҶ’- State management with @belt/tools 14вҶ’ 15вҶ’## Getting Started 16вҶ’ 17вҶ’```bash 18вҶ’# Install dependencies 19вҶ’bun install 20вҶ’ 21вҶ’# Start development server 22вҶ’bun dev 23вҶ’ 24вҶ’# Build for production 25вҶ’bun run build 26вҶ’ 27вҶ’# Lint code 28вҶ’bun run lint 29вҶ’ 30вҶ’# Format code 31вҶ’bun run format 32вҶ’``` 33вҶ’ 34вҶ’## Project Structure 35вҶ’ 36вҶ’``` 37вҶ’src/ 38вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.tsx # Entry point 39вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ App.tsx # Root component 40вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ store/ # @belt/tools state machines 41вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ engine/ # PM projection algorithm (CRITICAL) 42вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ import/ # CSV parsing and validation 43вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ canvas/ # deck.gl infinite canvas 44вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ components/ # React UI components 45вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ types/ # TypeScript interfaces 46вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ utils/ # Helpers 47вҶ’ 48вҶ’public/sample-data/ # Sample CSV files for testing 49вҶ’docs/ # 9-part specification (MMS_SPEC_001-009) 50вҶ’``` 51вҶ’ 52вҶ’--- 53вҶ’ 54вҶ’## @belt/tools Utilities Reference 55вҶ’ 56вҶ’The `@belt/tools` package provides comprehensive utilities for state management, runtime validation, and common operations. All utilities are already available in this project. 57вҶ’ 58вҶ’### State Machine 59вҶ’ 60вҶ’Immer-based reactive state management with React hooks and automatic cache management. 61вҶ’ 62вҶ’#### Creating Slices 63вҶ’ 64вҶ’```typescript 65вҶ’import { createSlice, createAsyncSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools'; 66вҶ’ 67вҶ’// Synchronous slice for local state 68вҶ’const fleetSlice = createSlice({ 69вҶ’ id: 'fleet', 70вҶ’ initial: { 71вҶ’ trains: [], 72вҶ’ pmHistory: [], 73вҶ’ selectedTrain: null as string | null, 74вҶ’ }, 75вҶ’ cache: { policy: 'session' }, // or 'public', 'private' 76вҶ’ reducers: { 77вҶ’ importFleet: (state, payload: Train[]) => { 78вҶ’ state.trains = payload; // Immer allows direct mutations 79вҶ’ }, 80вҶ’ selectTrain: (state, trainId: string) => { 81вҶ’ state.selectedTrain = trainId; 82вҶ’ }, 83вҶ’ clearSelection: (state) => { 84вҶ’ state.selectedTrain = null; 85вҶ’ }, 86вҶ’ }, 87вҶ’}); 88вҶ’ 89вҶ’// Async slice for data fetching 90вҶ’const analyticsSlice = createAsyncSlice({ 91вҶ’ id: 'analytics', 92вҶ’ cache: { 93вҶ’ policy: 'public', 94вҶ’ ttl: '5m', // Time-to-live using ms() format 95вҶ’ swr: true, // Stale-while-revalidate 96вҶ’ }, 97вҶ’ context: {}, 98вҶ’ load: async () => { 99вҶ’ const response = await fetch('/api/analytics'); 100вҶ’ return response.json(); 101вҶ’ }, 102вҶ’}); 103вҶ’``` 104вҶ’ 105вҶ’#### Creating Machines 106вҶ’ 107вҶ’```tsx 108вҶ’import { createMachine } from '@belt/tools'; 109вҶ’ 110вҶ’const appMachine = createMachine({ 111вҶ’ id: 'mms', 112вҶ’ slices: { 113вҶ’ fleet: fleetSlice, 114вҶ’ analytics: analyticsSlice, 115вҶ’ }, 116вҶ’ context: { userId: null }, // Global context 117вҶ’}); 118вҶ’ 119вҶ’// Use in React components 120вҶ’function FleetView() { 121вҶ’ const { state, actions } = appMachine.useMachine('fleet'); 122вҶ’ 123вҶ’ return ( 124вҶ’
125вҶ’ 128вҶ’
{state.trains.length} trains loaded
129вҶ’
130вҶ’ ); 131вҶ’} 132вҶ’``` 133вҶ’ 134вҶ’**Cache Policies:** 135вҶ’- `public` - Shared cache, TTL-based invalidation 136вҶ’- `private` - User-specific cache, invalidated on user change 137вҶ’- `session` - Session-specific cache, invalidated on app restart 138вҶ’ 139вҶ’--- 140вҶ’ 141вҶ’### Runtime Schema Validation 142вҶ’ 143вҶ’Type-safe runtime validation using TypeBox with concise syntax. 144вҶ’ 145вҶ’#### Basic Types 146вҶ’ 147вҶ’```typescript 148вҶ’import { createType, createSchema, isType, invariantType, InferType } from '@belt/tools'; 149вҶ’ 150вҶ’// Primitive types 151вҶ’const StringType = createType('string'); 152вҶ’const NumberType = createType('number'); 153вҶ’const BooleanType = createType('boolean'); 154вҶ’const DateType = createType('date'); 155вҶ’ 156вҶ’// Optional types (append ?) 157вҶ’const OptionalString = createType('string?'); 158вҶ’const OptionalNumber = createType('number?'); 159вҶ’ 160вҶ’// Array types (append []) 161вҶ’const StringArray = createType('string[]'); 162вҶ’const NumberArray = createType('number[]'); 163вҶ’ 164вҶ’// Type guards 165вҶ’if (isType(value, 'string')) { 166вҶ’ console.log(value.toUpperCase()); // TypeScript knows it's string 167вҶ’} 168вҶ’ 169вҶ’if (isType(value, 'date')) { 170вҶ’ console.log(value.toISOString()); // TypeScript knows it's Date 171вҶ’} 172вҶ’``` 173вҶ’ 174вҶ’#### Object Schemas 175вҶ’ 176вҶ’```typescript 177вҶ’import { createSchema, InferType } from '@belt/tools'; 178вҶ’ 179вҶ’// Define schema 180вҶ’const TrainModel = createSchema({ 181вҶ’ id: 'string', 182вҶ’ name: 'string', 183вҶ’ mileage: 'number', 184вҶ’ lastPM: 'date', 185вҶ’ tags: 'string[]', 186вҶ’ metadata: 'object?', // Optional generic object 187вҶ’}); 188вҶ’ 189вҶ’// Infer TypeScript type 190вҶ’type Train = InferType; 191вҶ’ 192вҶ’// Parse and validate 193вҶ’const train = TrainModel.parse({ 194вҶ’ id: 'T001', 195вҶ’ name: 'Red Line', 196вҶ’ mileage: 50000, 197вҶ’ lastPM: new Date('2025-01-01'), 198вҶ’ tags: ['metro', 'active'], 199вҶ’}); 200вҶ’ 201вҶ’// Validate without parsing 202вҶ’if (TrainModel.isValid(data)) { 203вҶ’ // TypeScript knows data is Train 204вҶ’} 205вҶ’ 206вҶ’// Assert type (throws if invalid) 207вҶ’invariantType(TrainModel, data); 208вҶ’``` 209вҶ’ 210вҶ’#### Complex Types 211вҶ’ 212вҶ’```typescript 213вҶ’import { createType } from '@belt/tools'; 214вҶ’ 215вҶ’// Nested objects 216вҶ’const PMEventType = createType({ 217вҶ’ properties: { 218вҶ’ trainId: 'string', 219вҶ’ scheduledDate: 'date', 220вҶ’ location: { 221вҶ’ properties: { 222вҶ’ facility: 'string', 223вҶ’ bay: 'number', 224вҶ’ }, 225вҶ’ }, 226вҶ’ tasks: { 227вҶ’ items: { // Array of objects 228вҶ’ properties: { 229вҶ’ code: 'string', 230вҶ’ duration: 'number', 231вҶ’ completed: 'boolean?', 232вҶ’ }, 233вҶ’ }, 234вҶ’ }, 235вҶ’ }, 236вҶ’}); 237вҶ’ 238вҶ’// Enums 239вҶ’const StatusEnum = createType({ 240вҶ’ enum: ['pending', 'in-progress', 'completed', 'cancelled'], 241вҶ’}); 242вҶ’ 243вҶ’// Unions 244вҶ’const IDType = createType({ 245вҶ’ union: ['string', 'number'], 246вҶ’}); 247вҶ’ 248вҶ’// Records (key-value maps) 249вҶ’const ConfigType = createType({ 250вҶ’ record: 'string', // Record 251вҶ’}); 252вҶ’``` 253вҶ’ 254вҶ’#### List Parsing 255вҶ’ 256вҶ’```typescript 257вҶ’// Parse array of items 258вҶ’const trainList = TrainModel.list().parse([ 259вҶ’ { id: 'T001', name: 'Red Line', mileage: 50000, lastPM: new Date(), tags: [] }, 260вҶ’ { id: 'T002', name: 'Blue Line', mileage: 30000, lastPM: new Date(), tags: [] }, 261вҶ’]); 262вҶ’ 263вҶ’// Parse array from nested path 264вҶ’const events = PMEventType.parseList(response, 'data.events'); 265вҶ’``` 266вҶ’ 267вҶ’--- 268вҶ’ 269вҶ’### Date Utilities 270вҶ’ 271вҶ’Date manipulation and formatting using date-fns. 272вҶ’ 273вҶ’```typescript 274вҶ’import { formatDate, setDefaultDateLocale, ms } from '@belt/tools'; 275вҶ’import { ptBR } from 'date-fns/locale'; 276вҶ’ 277вҶ’// Format dates 278вҶ’formatDate(new Date(), 'yyyy-MM-dd'); // '2026-01-14' 279вҶ’formatDate(new Date(), 'MMM dd, yyyy'); // 'Jan 14, 2026' 280вҶ’formatDate('2026-01-14', 'dd/MM/yyyy'); // '14/01/2026' 281вҶ’ 282вҶ’// Set global locale 283вҶ’setDefaultDateLocale(ptBR); 284вҶ’formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM'); // 'janeiro' 285вҶ’ 286вҶ’// Override locale for single call 287вҶ’import { enUS } from 'date-fns/locale'; 288вҶ’formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM', { locale: enUS }); // 'January' 289вҶ’ 290вҶ’// Time string conversion 291вҶ’ms('1s'); // 1000 292вҶ’ms('5m'); // 300000 293вҶ’ms('1h'); // 3600000 294вҶ’ms('2d'); // 172800000 295вҶ’ms('1w'); // 604800000 296вҶ’ms('1mo'); // ~2629800000 297вҶ’ms('1y'); // ~31557600000 298вҶ’ms('500ms'); // 500 299вҶ’ 300вҶ’// Use in configs 301вҶ’const CACHE_TTL = ms('5m'); 302вҶ’const DEBOUNCE = ms('300ms'); 303вҶ’const POLL_INTERVAL = ms('30s'); 304вҶ’``` 305вҶ’ 306вҶ’**Supported time units:** `ms`, `s`, `m`/`min`, `h`, `d`, `w`, `mo`/`mon`/`months`, `y` 307вҶ’ 308вҶ’--- 309вҶ’ 310вҶ’### Object Utilities 311вҶ’ 312вҶ’Type-safe object manipulation with deep path access. 313вҶ’ 314вҶ’```typescript 315вҶ’import { 316вҶ’ getProperty, 317вҶ’ setProperty, 318вҶ’ objectKeys, 319вҶ’ objectEntries, 320вҶ’ objectValues, 321вҶ’ keyBy, 322вҶ’ isObject, 323вҶ’ assertObject, 324вҶ’ requireAll, 325вҶ’ deepFreeze, 326вҶ’} from '@belt/tools'; 327вҶ’ 328вҶ’// Deep property access (type-safe) 329вҶ’const street = getProperty(user, 'address.street'); 330вҶ’const city = getProperty(user, 'address.city', true); // Allow falsy values 331вҶ’ 332вҶ’// Set nested properties 333вҶ’setProperty(train, 'metadata.lastInspection', new Date()); 334вҶ’ 335вҶ’// Object manipulation 336вҶ’const keys = objectKeys(train); // Type-safe keys 337вҶ’const entries = objectEntries(train); // Typed entries 338вҶ’const values = objectValues(train); // Typed values 339вҶ’ 340вҶ’// Array to object by key 341вҶ’const trainsById = keyBy(trains, 'id'); 342вҶ’// Or with function 343вҶ’const trainsByName = keyBy(trains, (t) => t.name); 344вҶ’ 345вҶ’// Type guards 346вҶ’if (isObject(value)) { 347вҶ’ // value is UnknownRecord 348вҶ’} 349вҶ’ 350вҶ’// Assert object with no falsy values 351вҶ’const validated = requireAll({ 352вҶ’ id: train.id, 353вҶ’ name: train.name, 354вҶ’ mileage: train.mileage, 355вҶ’}); 356вҶ’ 357вҶ’// Deep freeze (immutability) 358вҶ’const frozen = deepFreeze(config); 359вҶ’``` 360вҶ’ 361вҶ’**Path Examples:** 362вҶ’```typescript 363вҶ’type User = { 364вҶ’ id: string; 365вҶ’ profile: { 366вҶ’ address: { 367вҶ’ street: string; 368вҶ’ city: string; 369вҶ’ }; 370вҶ’ }; 371вҶ’}; 372вҶ’ 373вҶ’// All paths are type-checked 374вҶ’getProperty(user, 'id'); // вң“ string 375вҶ’getProperty(user, 'profile.address.city'); // вң“ string 376вҶ’getProperty(user, 'profile.invalid'); // вң— Type error 377вҶ’``` 378вҶ’ 379вҶ’--- 380вҶ’ 381вҶ’### String Utilities 382вҶ’ 383вҶ’String manipulation with type inference. 384вҶ’ 385вҶ’```typescript 386вҶ’import { capitalize, isString, assertString } from '@belt/tools'; 387вҶ’ 388вҶ’// Capitalize 389вҶ’capitalize('hello'); // 'Hello' (type: 'Hello') 390вҶ’ 391вҶ’// Type guards 392вҶ’if (isString(value)) { 393вҶ’ console.log(value.toUpperCase()); 394вҶ’} 395вҶ’ 396вҶ’// Assertions 397вҶ’assertString(value); 398вҶ’assertString(value, { notEmpty: true }); 399вҶ’assertString(value, { message: 'Name is required', notEmpty: true }); 400вҶ’``` 401вҶ’ 402вҶ’--- 403вҶ’ 404вҶ’### Error Handling 405вҶ’ 406вҶ’Comprehensive error utilities with stack trace management. 407вҶ’ 408вҶ’```typescript 409вҶ’import { 410вҶ’ invariant, 411вҶ’ tryCatch, 412вҶ’ InvariantError, 413вҶ’ createErrorList, 414вҶ’} from '@belt/tools'; 415вҶ’ 416вҶ’// Assertions 417вҶ’invariant(user.id, 'User ID is required'); 418вҶ’invariant(trains.length > 0, 'No trains found'); 419вҶ’ 420вҶ’// Safe error handling 421вҶ’const [error, result] = tryCatch(() => JSON.parse(data)); 422вҶ’if (error) { 423вҶ’ console.error('Parse failed:', error.message); 424вҶ’} else { 425вҶ’ console.log('Success:', result); 426вҶ’} 427вҶ’ 428вҶ’// Collect multiple errors 429вҶ’const errors = createErrorList('Validation failed: '); 430вҶ’if (!train.id) errors.add('Missing ID'); 431вҶ’if (!train.name) errors.add('Missing name'); 432вҶ’if (train.mileage < 0) errors.add('Invalid mileage'); 433вҶ’errors.check(); // Throws if any errors 434вҶ’``` 435вҶ’ 436вҶ’--- 437вҶ’ 438вҶ’### Type Utilities 439вҶ’ 440вҶ’Advanced TypeScript type helpers. 441вҶ’ 442вҶ’```typescript 443вҶ’import type { 444вҶ’ Paths, // All dot-notation paths of object 445вҶ’ PathType, // Type at specific path 446вҶ’ Compute, // Flatten intersection types 447вҶ’ Writable, // Remove readonly modifiers 448вҶ’ Falsy, // Union of falsy values 449вҶ’ InferType, // Infer type from schema 450вҶ’ UnknownRecord, // { [key: string]: unknown } 451вҶ’ AnyRecord, // { [key: string]: any } 452вҶ’ MaybePromise, // T | Promise 453вҶ’ MaybeArray, // T | T[] 454вҶ’ PromiseValue, // Extract Promise вҶ’ T 455вҶ’ Simplify, // Flatten complex types 456вҶ’ Merge, // Deep merge two types 457вҶ’ PartialFields, // Make specific fields optional 458вҶ’ NullableToPartial, // Convert nullable to optional 459вҶ’ UnionToIntersection, // Union вҶ’ Intersection 460вҶ’ CastUnknown, // Cast if unknown 461вҶ’ SerializableOnly, // Only serializable fields 462вҶ’} from '@belt/tools'; 463вҶ’ 464вҶ’// Example: Paths 465вҶ’type User = { 466вҶ’ id: string; 467вҶ’ profile: { 468вҶ’ address: { 469вҶ’ street: string; 470вҶ’ }; 471вҶ’ }; 472вҶ’}; 473вҶ’ 474вҶ’type UserPaths = Paths; 475вҶ’// 'id' | 'profile' | 'profile.address' | 'profile.address.street' 476вҶ’ 477вҶ’type StreetType = PathType; 478вҶ’// string 479вҶ’ 480вҶ’// Example: Compute (flatten intersections) 481вҶ’type A = { a: number }; 482вҶ’type B = { b: string }; 483вҶ’type Flat = Compute; // { a: number; b: string } 484вҶ’ 485вҶ’// Example: Writable 486вҶ’type ReadOnly = { readonly id: string; readonly name: string }; 487вҶ’type Mutable = Writable; // { id: string; name: string } 488вҶ’ 489вҶ’// Example: PartialFields 490вҶ’type Train = { id: string; name: string; metadata: object }; 491вҶ’type Optional = PartialFields; 492вҶ’// { id: string; name: string; metadata?: object } 493вҶ’``` 494вҶ’ 495вҶ’**Type Guards:** 496вҶ’```typescript 497вҶ’import { isFalsy } from '@belt/tools'; 498вҶ’import type { Falsy } from '@belt/tools'; 499вҶ’ 500вҶ’// All falsy values 501вҶ’const falsyValues: Falsy = false | '' | null | undefined | 0 | 0n | NaN; 502вҶ’ 503вҶ’if (isFalsy(value)) { 504вҶ’ // value is one of the falsy values 505вҶ’} 506вҶ’``` 507вҶ’ 508вҶ’--- 509вҶ’ 510вҶ’### Other Utilities 511вҶ’ 512вҶ’```typescript 513вҶ’import { delay, maskData, getTypeOf, createProxy } from '@belt/tools'; 514вҶ’ 515вҶ’// Async delay 516вҶ’await delay(500); // Wait 500ms 517вҶ’await delay(); // Default 500ms 518вҶ’ 519вҶ’// Mask sensitive data for logging 520вҶ’const masked = maskData({ 521вҶ’ password: 'secret123', 522вҶ’ email: 'user@example.com', 523вҶ’ nested: { token: 'abc123' }, 524вҶ’}); 525вҶ’// { password: 'String', email: 'String', nested: { token: 'String' } } 526вҶ’ 527вҶ’// Get type name 528вҶ’getTypeOf(null); // 'Null' 529вҶ’getTypeOf(undefined); // 'Undefined' 530вҶ’getTypeOf(new Date()); // 'Date' 531вҶ’getTypeOf({}); // 'Object' 532вҶ’getTypeOf([]); // 'Array' 533вҶ’getTypeOf(NaN); // 'NaN' 534вҶ’ 535вҶ’// Lazy initialization 536вҶ’const lazyConfig = createProxy(() => loadConfig()); 537вҶ’// Config is only loaded on first access 538вҶ’``` 539вҶ’ 540вҶ’ 541вҶ’ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
